title: What would Angels do without Lilith
display_title: What would Angels do without Lilith
wikitext:
[]: What would Angels do without Lilith?             Quote Post by Magician_of_Oz » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:32 pm Ok so this is my first relevant topic on any Eva forum and would like to bring up a question that has been bugging me for some time. What if Lilith hadn't accidentally come to Earth? If I've interpreted the Dead Sea Scrolls correctly then Adam would have made the same Angels we know and love either way, but what would they have done? Life as we know it spend most of it's time eating and reproducing but S^2 engine probably eliminates the need to eat and as far as I know Angels do not reproduce so what would they do? Arael seems to enjoy probing human minds but would she receive the same satisfaction from probing the minds of the other Angels (probably not because they lack the fruit of knowledge) and how would Armisael act without an Eva around, would she try to merge with another Angel?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:39 pm Eh, they'd probably just wander around the planet like big dumb happy cows and be totally okay with having no real purpose other than to exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Magician_of_Oz » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:55 pm NemZ wrote: Eh, they'd probably just wander around the planet like big dumb happy cows and be totally okay with having no real purpose other than to exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:10 pm Magician_of_Oz wrote: So then only beings with the fruit of knowledge can have any sort of purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:11 pm Maybe they do, maybe they don't. As fruit of knowledge type beings, whatever that purpose may or may not be probably would never make any sense to us, or else be so obvious that we'd dismiss it as a purpose in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:19 pm Fair enough. I just don't think that there's any important difference between your description of Angels and the way humans are shown to behave in Eva - except that humans are more fond of verbalizing their complaints with it. Honestly though, it's pretty obvious in the series that both humans and Angels have the power and drive to become something "greater" than what they are, although humans take a while to achieve that power. The Angels clearly feel that their condition is an imperfect and undesirable one, at least as we observe them in NGE - perhaps that's a consequence of their unnatural birth, or perhaps that's just the Angelic condition. Who knows, maybe in the absence of Lilith and Lilim they'd all just waltz straight back to Mommy; maybe the whole Fruit of Life-type being is a bust in the long run and the FAR just didn't realize it beforehand.

[]:             Quote Post by Magician_of_Oz » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:27 pm Azathoth wrote: The Angels clearly feel that their condition is an imperfect and undesirable one, at least as we observe them in NGE - perhaps that's a consequence of their unnatural birth, or perhaps that's just the Angelic condition. Who knows, maybe in the absence of Lilith and Lilim they'd all just waltz straight back to Mommy; maybe the whole Fruit of Life-type being is a bust in the long run and the FAR just didn't realize it beforehand.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 29, 2010 11:19 am Previous topic on the same basic subject, which I seem to recall being pretty nifty. You can find a bunch of other stuff by bringing up the thread list through the navbar and looking for key words in the thread titles.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Dec 31, 2010 6:20 am They'd probably spend all their times looking for the FoK like we did trying to attain the FoL. Grass is always greener and whatnot. Ahhh, to be a happy cow.

[]:             Quote Post by Magician_of_Oz » Fri Dec 31, 2010 4:40 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: They'd probably spend all their times looking for the FoK like we did trying to attain the FoL. Grass is always greener and whatnot. Ahhh, to be a happy cow.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:06 pm Magician_of_Oz wrote: To obtain both Fruits and become like God. The theory sounds good but Lilim wouldn't have knowledge of the fruit of life if it weren't for the fact that Earth had both Adam and Lilith so I don't think the Angels would've known about the opposite fruit if it had been just Adam on Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Magician_of_Oz » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:38 pm Reichu wrote: Thus, were Earth instead populated by a type of humanity unbound by mortality or physical limits -- in this case, Adam's children -- by their very nature as humans they would almost invariably long for what they do not have. And this would be the "Fruit of Knowledge" (whatever exactly that is) that Lilin possess and require by virtue of their limitations.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 01, 2011 6:29 am Reichu wrote: Thus, were Earth instead populated by a type of humanity unbound by mortality or physical limits -- in this case, Adam's children -- by their very nature as humans they would almost invariably long for what they do not have.
